matrix.behavior: select_top_k picks correct top-3 by descending score from 5 candidates
expand_insufficient_topics calls GraphNeighbors exactly once, only for the weak topic, hops=DEFAULT_EXPANSION_HOPS
combine_and_cap enforces k+2k=9 cap end-to-end with oversized neighbor pool, selected topics prioritized
When no selected topic is insufficient, expand_insufficient_topics returns [] and issues zero GraphNeighbors calls
combine_and_cap with empty expansions returns exactly the selected file_ids, under cap
Two independently-insufficient topics each trigger exactly one GraphNeighbors call with correct file_id/hops
A neighbor file_id shared by two different expansions appears exactly once in combine_and_cap's output
A neighbor file_id colliding with a directly-selected topic's file_id appears exactly once, priority to selected
Final result length equals distinct file_id count (dedup, not the k+2k cap, is the limiting mechanism in this scenario)

coverage_summary: 3 scenarios / 9 assertion blocks cover full pipeline composition (select_top_k -> expand_insufficient_topics -> combine_and_cap) for: expansion-triggered + cap enforcement, expansion-absent path, and dedup (both neighbor-neighbor and selected-neighbor collision) surfaced end-to-end.
out_of_scope_covered_elsewhere: select_top_k unit edge cases (negative k, empty input, ties) -- test_topic_selector.py
is_insufficient_alone / expand_insufficient_topics unit edge cases (hops validation, ratio validation) -- test_topic_selector_expansion.py
combine_and_cap unit edge cases (negative k, empty inputs, ordering) -- test_topic_selector_cap.py
